stages.objective: Someone on the docks remembers the clerk who stopped coming to work. Ask around - the Mission pot, the Beaching Strand, the Wrackhouse counter, or the rooming house that still keeps his bed.
stages.log: Dock talk named the counting-house clerk who vanished the night a ledger line was erased: Tam Sedge, lodged four doors from the desk he never came back to.

stages.objective: Ottavan Crell knows which line was erased. Ask him at the Weighhouse.
stages.log: Crell said it plain enough: one line gone from the King's ledger, and the clerk who copied it gone the same night.

stages.objective: Reach the clerk's desk inside the Counting-House. Its door answers to the Watch's colors - wear a face it respects, dash the Watch's grace, or lift Master Gilt's vault key.
stages.log: Got past the Counting-House door, to the desk the clerk left behind.

stages.objective: The clerk's desk is still his. Open it.
stages.log: The locked drawer gave up a torn ledger leaf - the copied line, in the clerk's own hand.

stages.objective: The torn leaf names the night. Crell would raise it. Gilt would bury it. The rooftops would trade on it.
stages.log: The torn leaf found its keeper.

stages.objective: Done. The Harbormaster holds the proof, and the Watch knows who brought it. Master Gilt will not forget the face that raised it.
stages.log: Crell took the leaf to the Weighhouse scales. The erased line stands raised, and the King's banker remembers who raised it.

stages.objective: Done. The banker paid Royals for a buried truth, and the Watch and the Temple both mark the smell of it.
stages.log: Gilt fed the leaf to a candle and counted out forty Royals. The line stays erased; the clerk stays a rumor.

stages.objective: Done. The rooftops keep the receipt now, and the brotherhood remembers who brought it up the wall.
stages.log: Finch folded the leaf away without reading it twice. The rooftops keep receipts, and this one is banked.

notes: S3 'The Vanished Clerk' - the game's first authored quest. Fiction seed: the gilt-crell-erasure micro-history (histories.json). The clerk never spawns (roster stays 692): he is the locked desk, the torn leaf, and the people who remember him - canon name Tam Sedge (DOCKS-GAZETTEER 4.6). World S3 pass folded in the Sim phase's declared cut: the 'sedge' landlady party + her rumor trigger (both lists append-only) and the prose polish. Quest talk tables live in barks.json under quest.vanished-clerk.* (key vocabulary linted by DocksBarkContentTest against THIS file's stages x parties). The pay effect seizes-what-exists; the bake tops Gilt's pocket to cover it.
